info.title: Alteriom Webhook Connector
info.description:
# Alteriom Webhook Connector API

Lightweight webhook connector that receives GitHub App webhooks, persists events, and dispatches them to external AI agents via HTTP POST and WebSocket.

## Key Features

- **Event Persistence** — All GitHub webhook events are stored in PostgreSQL with full payload
- **HTTP Dispatch** — Queued delivery with retries and exponential backoff via BullMQ
- **WebSocket Streaming** — Real-time event push via Socket.IO on `/ws`
- **V2 Advanced Filtering** — Compound event.action subscriptions, glob patterns, payload conditions
- **Event Batching** — Aggregate rapid-fire events into batches
- **HMAC Verification** — Timing-safe signature verification for both GitHub and subscriber delivery

## Authentication

- **API endpoints** (`/api/*`): Bearer token authentication with the configured API_KEY
- **Webhook endpoint** (`/webhooks/github`): GitHub HMAC-SHA256 signature verification
- **Dashboard** (`/`): Basic HTTP authentication

## WebSocket (Socket.IO)

Connect to `/ws` with Socket.IO:

```javascript
const socket = io('http://<host>', {
  path: '/ws',
  auth: { apiKey: '<API_KEY>', name: 'my-agent' }
});
socket.on('webhook-event', (event) => { /* ... */ });
```

## Rate Limiting

- Webhook endpoint: 120 requests/minute
- API endpoints: No rate limit (API key required)

tags.name: Subscribers (V2)
tags.description: Advanced subscriber management with compound events, glob filters, payload conditions, and batching

tags.name: Events
tags.description: Webhook event querying, delivery tracking, and replay

BearerAuth.description: API key passed as Bearer token. Set via API_KEY environment variable.

include.description: Patterns to include (allowlist). Supports glob wildcards (*)

exclude.description: Patterns to exclude (blocklist). Supports glob wildcards (*)

IncludeExcludeFilter.description: Include/exclude filter with glob pattern support

field.description: Dot-notation path into the GitHub payload

value.description: Value to compare against. Not required for "exists" operator.

PayloadCondition.description: Payload field condition for deep filtering

max_batch_size.description: Flush immediately when batch reaches this size

post.description: Create an HTTP subscriber that will receive webhook events. V1 API supports simple event filtering.
Re-dispatch a stored event to matching subscribers (or a specific subscriber)

subscriber_id.description: Replay to a specific subscriber only

get.summary: Get subscriber details with delivery stats

delete.description: Soft-deactivate by default. Use ?hard=true to permanently delete.

post.summary: Register a V2 subscriber with advanced filtering
post.description: Create a subscriber with compound event.action subscriptions, include/exclude glob filters, payload conditions, and optional event batching.

get.summary: List all subscribers with filter version annotation
Get V2 filter documentation and examples
get.description: Returns comprehensive documentation of all available V2 filter options with examples and common configurations.

get.summary: Get V2 subscriber details with stats

post.summary: Send test ping to V2 subscriber

post.description: Main GitHub webhook receiver. Verifies X-Hub-Signature-256, persists events, routes through handlers, and dispatches to subscribers. Rate limited to 120 req/min.

get.description: Returns event/delivery stats, subscriber count, WebSocket client count, and uptime.
Aggregated stats for the dashboard: event/delivery counts, subscribers, WebSocket clients, repo breakdown.
Returns event counts bucketed by hour over a time period. Used for dashboard charts.
Returns full event data including payload, headers, and all delivery attempts.
Comprehensive health check of all system components (PostgreSQL, Redis, BullMQ). Returns 503 if unhealthy.
Exposes Prometheus-compatible metrics for monitoring (event counts, delivery latencies, queue depths).
